volumeTitle: Birds. Lysistrata. Women at the Thesmophoria
label: Birds (Section 1.1270 - Section 1396)

sections.label: Section 1.1270
sections.original: οἰχόμενον , εἰ μηδέποτε νοστήσει πάλιν .
sections.translation: we sent to the mortals has never returned .

sections.label: Section 1.1271
sections.original: ὦ Πισθέταιρʼ ὦ μακάριʼ ὦ σοφώτατε ,
sections.translation: Oh ! blessed Pisthetaerus , very wise , very illustrious , very gracious , thrice happy , very ... Come , prompt me , somebody , do

sections.label: Section 1.1273b
sections.original: τί σὺ λέγεις ;
sections.translation: Get to your story !

sections.label: Section 1.1274
sections.original: στεφάνῳ σε χρυσῷ τῷδε σοφίας οὕνεκα
sections.translation: All peoples are filled with admiration for your wisdom , and they award you this golden crown .

sections.label: Section 1.1276
sections.original: δέχομαι . τί δʼ οὕτως οἱ λεῲ τιμῶσί με ;
sections.translation: I accept it . But tell me , why do the people admire me ?

sections.label: Section 1.1277
sections.original: ὦ κλεινοτάτην αἰθέριον οἰκίσας πόλιν ,
sections.translation: Oh you , who have founded so illustrious a city in the air , you know not in what esteem men hold you and how many there are who burn with desire to dwell in it .

sections.label: Section 1.1280
sections.original: πρὶν μὲν γὰρ οἰκίσαι σε τήνδε τὴν πόλιν ,
sections.translation: Before your city was built , all men had a mania for Sparta ; long hair and fasting were held in honor , men went dirty like Socrates and carried staves . Now all is changed .

sections.label: Section 1.1285
sections.original: ποιοῦσιν ἅπερ ὄρνιθες ἐκμιμούμενοι ·
sections.translation: Firstly , as soon as it ' s dawn , they all spring out of bed together to go and seek their food , the same as you do ; then they fly off towards the notices and finally devour the decrees .

sections.label: Section 1.1290
sections.original: ὠρνιθομάνουν δʼ οὕτω περιφανῶς ὥστε καὶ
sections.translation: The bird-madness is so clear that many actually bear the names of birds . There is a halting victualler , who styles himself the partridge ; Menippus calls himself the swallow ; Opuntius the one-eyed crow ;

sections.label: Section 1.1295
sections.original: κορυδὸς Φιλοκλέει , χηναλώπηξ Θεογένει ,
sections.translation: Philocles the lark ; Theogenes the fox-goose ; Lycurgus the ibis ; Chaerephon the bat ; Syracosius the magpie ; Midias the quail ; indeed he looks like a quail that has been hit hard on the head .

sections.label: Section 1.1300
sections.original: ᾖδον δʼ ὑπὸ φιλορνιθίας πάντες μέλη ,
sections.translation: Out of love for the birds they repeat all the songs which concern the swallow , the teal , the goose or the pigeon ; in each verse you see wings , or at all events a few feathers . This is what is happening down there .

sections.label: Section 1.1305
sections.original: ἥξουσʼ ἐκεῖθεν δεῦρο πλεῖν ἢ μύριοι
sections.translation: Finally , there are more than ten thousand folk who are coming here from earth to ask you for feathers and hooked claws ; so , mind you supply yourself with wings for the immigrants .

sections.label: Section 1.1308
sections.original: οὐκ ἆρα μὰ Δίʼ ἡμῖν ἔτʼ ἔργον ἑστάναι .
sections.translation: Ah ! by Zeus , there ' s no time for idling . To some slaves . Go as quick as possible and fill every hamper ,

sections.label: Section 1.1310
sections.original: καὶ τοὺς κοφίνους ἅπαντας ἐμπίμπλη πτερῶν ·
sections.translation: every basket you can find with wings . Manes will bring them to me outside the walls , where I will welcome those who present themselves .

sections.label: Section 1.1313
sections.original: ταχὺ δὴ πολυάνορα τάνδε πόλιν
sections.translation: This town will soon be inhabited by a crowd of men .

sections.label: Section 1.1315
sections.original: τύχη μόνον προσείη .
sections.translation: Fortune favours us alone

sections.label: Section 1.1316
sections.original: κατέχουσι δʼ ἔρωτες ἐμᾶς πόλεως .
sections.translation: and thus they have fallen in love with our city .

sections.label: Section 1.1317
sections.original: θάττον φέρειν κελεύω .
sections.translation: Come , hurry up and bring them along .

sections.label: Section 1.1318
sections.original: τί γὰρ οὐκ ἔνι ταύτῃ
sections.translation: Will not man find here everything that can please him —

sections.label: Section 1.1320
sections.original: Σοφία Πόθος Ἀμβροσία Χάριτες
sections.translation: wisdom , love , the divine Graces , the sweet face of gentle peace ?

sections.label: Section 1.1323
sections.original: ὡς βλακικῶς διακονεῖς ·
sections.translation: Oh ! you lazy servant ! won ' t you hurry yourself ?

sections.label: Section 1.1325
sections.original: φερέτω κάλαθον ταχύ τις πτερύγων ,
sections.translation: Let a basket of wings be brought speedily .

sections.label: Section 1.1327
sections.original: τύπτων γε τοῦτον ὡδί .
sections.translation: Come , beat him as I do , and put some life into him ;

sections.label: Section 1.1328
sections.original: πάνυ γὰρ βραδύς ἐστί τις ὥσπερ ὄνος .
sections.translation: he is as lazy as an ass .

sections.label: Section 1.1329
sections.original: Μανῆς γάρ ἐστι δειλός .
sections.translation: Aye , Manes is a great craven .

sections.label: Section 1.1330
sections.original: σὺ δὲ τὰ πτερὰ πρῶτον
sections.translation: Begin by putting this heap of wings in order ; divide them in three parts according to the birds from whom they came ; the singing , the prophetic and the aquatic birds ; then you must take care to distribute them to the men according to their character .

sections.label: Section 1335
sections.original: οὔ τοι μὰ τὰς κερχνῇδας ἔτι σοῦ σχήσομαι ,
sections.translation: Oh ! by the kestrels ! I can keep my hands off you no longer ; you are too slow and lazy altogether .

sections.label: Section 1337
sections.original: γενοίμαν αἰετὸς ὑψιπέτας ,
sections.translation: Oh ! might I but become an eagle , who soars in the skies ! Oh ! might I fly above the azure waves of the barren sea !

sections.label: Section 1340
sections.original: ἔοικεν οὐ ψευδαγγελήσειν ἅγγελος .
sections.translation: Ha ! it would seem the news was true ; I hear someone coming who talks of wings .

sections.label: Section 1342
sections.original: αἰβοῖ ·
sections.translation: Nothing is more charming than to fly ; I am bird-mad and fly towards you , for I want

sections.label: Section 1345
sections.original: οἰκεῖν μεθʼ ὑμῶν κἀπιθυμῶ τῶν νόμων .
sections.translation: to live with you and to obey your laws .

sections.label: Section 1346
sections.original: ποίων νόμων ; πολλοὶ γὰρ ὀρνίθων νόμοι .
sections.translation: Which laws ? The birds have many laws .

sections.label: Section 1347
sections.original: πάντων · μάλιστα δʼ ὅτι καλὸν νομίζεται
sections.translation: All of them ; but the one that pleases me most is that among the birds it is considered a fine thing to peck and strangle one ' s father .

sections.label: Section 1349
sections.original: καὶ νὴ Δίʼ ἀνδρεῖόν γε πάνυ νομιζομεν ,
sections.translation: Yes , by Zeus ! according to us ,

sections.label: Section 1350
sections.original: ὃς ἂν πεπλήγῃ τὸν πατέρα νεοττὸς ὤν .
sections.translation: he who dares to strike his father , while still a chick , is a brave fellow .

sections.label: Section 1351
sections.original: διὰ ταῦτα μέντοι δεῦρʼ ἀνοικισθεὶς ἐγὼ
sections.translation: And therefore I want to dwell here , for I want to strangle my father and inherit his wealth .

sections.label: Section 1353
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἔστιν ἡμῖν τοῖσιν ὄρνισιν νόμος
sections.translation: But we have also an ancient law written in the code of the storks , which runs thus ,

sections.label: Section 1355
sections.original: ἐπὴν ὁ πατὴρ ὁ πελαργὸς ἐκπετησίμους
sections.translation: When the stork father has reared his young and has taught them to fly , the young must in their turn support the father .

sections.label: Section 1358
sections.original: ἀπέλαυσά τἄρα νὴ Δίʼ ἐλθὼν ἐνθαδί ,
sections.translation: It ' s hardly worth while coming all this distance to be compelled to keep my father !

sections.label: Section 1360
sections.original: οὐδέν γʼ . ἐπειδήπερ γὰρ ἦλθες ὦ μέλε
sections.translation: No , no , young friend , since you have come to us with such willingness , I am going to give you these black wings , as though you were an orphan bird ; furthermore , some good advice , that I received myself in infancy . Don ' t strike your father , but take

sections.label: Section 1365
sections.original: τὴν πτέρυγα καὶ τουτὶ τὸ πλῆκτρον θἀτέρᾳ ,
sections.translation: these wings in one hand and these spurs in the other ; imagine you have a cock ' s crest on your head and go and mount guard and fight ; live on your pay and respect your father ' s life . You ' re a gallant fellow ! Very well , then ! Fly to Thrace and fight .

sections.label: Section 1370
sections.original: νὴ τὸν Διόνυσον εὖ γέ μοι δοκεῖς λέγειν ,
sections.translation: By Bacchus ! You ' re right ; I will follow your counsel .

sections.label: Section 1371b
sections.original: νοῦν ἄρʼ ἔξεις νὴ Δία .
sections.translation: It ' s acting wisely , by Zeus ..

sections.label: Section 1373
sections.original: ἀναπέτομαι δὴ πρὸς Ὄλυμπον πτερύγεσσι κούφαις ·
sections.translation: On my light pinions I soar off to Olympus ; in its capricious flight my Muse flutters along the thousand paths of poetry in turn ...

sections.label: Section 1375
sections.original: τουτὶ τὸ πρᾶγμα φορτίου δεῖται πτερῶν .
sections.translation: This is a fellow will need a whole shipload of wings .

sections.label: Section 1376
sections.original: ἀφόβῳ φρενὶ σώματί τε νέαν ἐφέπων —
sections.translation: ... and being fearless and vigorous , it is seeking fresh outlet .

sections.label: Section 1377
sections.original: ἀσπαζόμεσθα φιλύρινον Κινησίαν .
sections.translation: Welcome , Cinesias , you lime-wood man ! Why have you come here twisting your game leg in circles ?

sections.label: Section 1380
sections.original: ὄρνις γενέσθαι βούλομαι λιγύφθογγος ἀηδών .
sections.translation: I want to become a bird , a tuneful nightingale .

sections.label: Section 1381
sections.original: παῦσαι μελῳδῶν , ἀλλʼ ὅ τι λέγεις εἰπέ μοι .
sections.translation: Enough of that sort of ditty . Tell me what you want .

sections.label: Section 1382
sections.original: ὑπὸ σοῦ πτερωθεὶς βούλομαι μετάρσιος
sections.translation: Give me wings and I will fly into the topmost airs to gather fresh songs in the clouds ,

sections.label: Section 1385
sections.original: ἀεροδονήτους καὶ νιφοβόλους ἀναβολάς .
sections.translation: in the midst of the vapors and the fleecy snow .

sections.label: Section 1386
sections.original: ἐκ τῶν νεφελῶν γὰρ ἄν τις ἀναβολὰς λάβοι ;
sections.translation: Gather songs in the clouds ?

sections.label: Section 1387
sections.original: κρέμαται μὲν οὖν ἐντεῦθεν ἡμῶν ἡ τέχνη .
sections.translation: ' Tis on them the whole of our latter-day art depends . The most brilliant dithyrambs are those that flap their wings in empty space and are clothed in mist and dense obscurity .

sections.label: Section 1390
sections.original: καὶ πτεροδόνητα · σὺ δὲ κλύων εἴσει τάχα .
sections.translation: To appreciate this , just listen .

sections.label: Section 1391
sections.original: οὐ δῆτʼ ἔγωγε .
sections.translation: Oh ! no , no , no !

sections.label: Section 1391b
sections.original: νὴ τὸν Ἡρακλέα σύ γε .
sections.translation: By Hermes ! but indeed you shall .

sections.label: Section 1392
sections.original: ἅπαντα γὰρ δίειμί σοι τὸν ἀέρα .
sections.translation: I shall travel through thine ethereal empire

sections.label: Section 1393
sections.original: εἴδωλα πετεινῶν
sections.translation: like a winged bird , who cleaveth space with his long neck ...

sections.label: Section 1395a
sections.original: ὠόπ .
sections.translation: Stop ! Way enough !

sections.label: Section 1396
sections.original: τὸν ἁλάδρομον ἁλάμενος
sections.translation: ... as I soar over the seas , carried by the breath of the winds ...
